sections.title: Section I - Multiple Choice
sections.directions: Each of the questions or incomplete statements below is followed by several suggested answers or completions. Select the one that is best in each case.

options.text: 1.6 Questions 20-21 In the system of two blocks and a spring shown above, blocks 1 and 2 are connected by a string that passes over a pulley. The initially unstretched spring connects block 1 to a rigid wall. Block 1 is released from rest, initially slides to the right, and is eventually brought to rest by the spring and by friction on the horizontal surface.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is true of the energy of the system during this process?

options.text: The total mechanical energy of the system is conserved.
The total mechanical energy of the system increases.
The energy lost to friction is equal to the gain in the potential energy of the spring.
The potential energy lost by block 2 is less in magnitude than the potential energy gained by the spring.
The potential energy lost by block 2 is greater in magnitude than the potential energy gained by the spring.
